displayName: Durable Power of Attorney (Estate Planning · Massachusetts)
template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY**:
Instrument authorizing an Agent (attorney-in-fact) to act on behalf of the Principal in financial and legal matters. 'Durable' because the authority survives the Principal's later incapacity. Governed by M.G.L. c. 190B, Article V, Part 5 (the Uniform Durable Power of Attorney Act as adopted by Massachusetts). Target length: 4–8 pages. Notarization is required.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `effectiveness`: `immediate` (default — effective upon execution) | `springing` (effective only upon the Principal's incapacity, certified by two licensed physicians).
- `scope`: `general` (default — full authority over all financial matters) | `limited` (specific transactions or time period only).
- `successorAgents`: integer — number of successor agents to name (typically 1–3).
- `includeGiftingPower`: boolean — when true, explicitly authorize the Agent to make gifts (required to satisfy the 'hot powers' rule — absent express authority, the Agent cannot gift, change beneficiary designations, or exercise fiduciary powers).
- `includeTrustAmendmentPower`: boolean — when true, authorize the Agent to fund and amend the Principal's Revocable Trust.
- `includeMedicaidPlanning`: boolean — when true, include specific authority for Medicaid / MassHealth planning (transfers, trust funding, annuity conversions).

**STRUCTURE**:

## DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY

### I. IDENTIFICATION
I, [Principal full legal name], of [city/town], [County] County, Commonwealth of Massachusetts ('Principal'), being of sound mind, do hereby make, constitute, and appoint [Primary Agent full legal name], of [address] ('Agent'), as my attorney-in-fact to act for me and in my name in the capacity and to the extent set forth below.

### II. SUCCESSOR AGENTS
If [Primary Agent] is unable or unwilling to serve, I appoint [First Successor Agent name] as my successor attorney-in-fact. If [First Successor] is also unable or unwilling, I appoint [Second Successor Agent name]. A successor shall have the same authority as the primary Agent.

Only one Agent may act at a time unless I have expressly authorized co-agents to act jointly or severally. Authority of a successor becomes effective upon the resignation, death, incapacity, or refusal to act of the prior Agent, as evidenced by a written statement signed by the successor and attached to this Power of Attorney.

### III. DURABILITY
This Power of Attorney is DURABLE and is not affected by my subsequent disability or incapacity, consistent with M.G.L. c. 190B, § 5-501. It shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by me in writing, until my death, or until terminated by operation of law.

### IV. EFFECTIVENESS
[Based on `effectiveness`]:

- **immediate**: This Power of Attorney is effective immediately upon execution and shall continue in effect until revoked, terminated, or until my death.

- **springing**: This Power of Attorney shall become effective only upon a written determination by two (2) licensed physicians, at least one of whom is my primary care physician, that I am unable to manage my financial affairs. The physicians' written determination shall be attached to this Power of Attorney. The Agent's authority continues until my capacity is restored (certified in writing by two physicians) or until this instrument is revoked.

### V. GRANT OF AUTHORITY — GENERAL POWERS
[Based on `scope`]:

- **general**: I grant to my Agent the full authority to act for me in all matters relating to:

(a) **Real estate** — to purchase, sell, exchange, lease (including leases exceeding three years), mortgage, manage, improve, insure, and otherwise deal with real property, and to execute and deliver deeds, mortgages, leases, and all related instruments;
(b) **Tangible personal property** — to purchase, sell, and manage tangible personal property;
(c) **Financial accounts** — to open, close, maintain, and transact business with respect to checking, savings, certificates of deposit, money market, and brokerage accounts at any financial institution; to deposit, withdraw, and transfer funds; to endorse and negotiate checks and other instruments; to make electronic transfers; to access safe-deposit boxes;
(d) **Investments** — to buy, sell, exchange, pledge, and otherwise manage securities, mutual funds, and other investments; to exercise options and voting rights; to manage brokerage accounts;
(e) **Business operations** — to manage any business interest I may own or control;
(f) **Claims and litigation** — to assert, defend, settle, and compromise claims and litigation in my name;
(g) **Tax matters** — to prepare, execute, and file federal, state, and local tax returns, to deal with the Internal Revenue Service and the Massachusetts Department of Revenue, and to represent me in all tax matters (this authority includes signing IRS Form 2848);
(h) **Insurance** — to purchase, maintain, modify, and cancel insurance policies; to file and settle claims;
(i) **Retirement plans** — to access, manage, and make elections regarding retirement plans, IRAs, 401(k) plans, and similar vehicles, including rollovers and required minimum distributions;
(j) **Government benefits** — to apply for and manage Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid/MassHealth, veterans' benefits, and other government benefits;
(k) **Digital assets** — full authority over my digital assets and the content of electronic communications, consistent with the Massachusetts Revised Uniform Fiduciary Access to Digital Assets Act, M.G.L. c. 190B, Article VII, and constituting my lawful consent under 18 U.S.C. §§ 2701 et seq.;
(l) **Borrowing and lending** — to borrow money and pledge assets, and to make loans on my behalf to the extent consistent with my estate plan.

### VI. SPECIAL POWERS (HOT POWERS) — EXPRESSLY GRANTED
[If `includeGiftingPower`]: I expressly authorize my Agent to make gifts of my property to any individual or charity, including to the Agent himself/herself, subject to the following limitation: annual gifts per donee shall not exceed the federal gift tax annual exclusion amount under 26 U.S.C. § 2503(b) (currently $18,000 per donee for 2024, adjusted for inflation) unless I have previously directed otherwise. Gifts may include direct payments of tuition and medical expenses that qualify under 26 U.S.C. § 2503(e).

[If `includeTrustAmendmentPower`]: I expressly authorize my Agent to transfer my assets to the [Grantor Name] Revocable Trust dated [date], to withdraw assets from such Trust, and to amend, restate, or revoke such Trust in any manner the Agent deems appropriate to fulfill my estate planning objectives.

[If `includeMedicaidPlanning`]: I expressly authorize my Agent to engage in Medicaid / MassHealth planning transactions, including without limitation: (a) transfers of my assets to family members, irrevocable trusts, or other transferees, including transfers that may result in a period of ineligibility under 42 U.S.C. § 1396p(c); (b) conversion of countable assets to exempt assets; (c) purchase of Medicaid-compliant annuities; (d) spend-down of resources on exempt expenditures; (e) applications for MassHealth benefits.

I authorize the Agent to change beneficiary designations on my financial accounts, retirement plans, and life insurance policies to carry out my estate planning objectives as reflected in my Will and/or Trust.

### VII. STANDARDS OF CONDUCT
My Agent shall:
(a) Act in good faith;
(b) Act loyally for my benefit;
(c) Act so as not to create a conflict of interest that impairs the Agent's ability to act impartially;
(d) Act with care, competence, and diligence ordinarily exercised by agents in similar circumstances;
(e) Keep a record of all receipts, disbursements, and transactions made on my behalf;
(f) Cooperate with any person who has authority to make healthcare decisions for me;
(g) Preserve my estate plan, to the extent actually known by the Agent, if preserving the plan is consistent with the Agent's duties.

### VIII. COMPENSATION AND REIMBURSEMENT
The Agent shall be entitled to reimbursement for reasonable expenses incurred on my behalf. [If the Agent is a professional: The Agent shall be entitled to reasonable compensation.] [If the Agent is a family member: The Agent shall serve without compensation.]

### IX. ACCOUNTING
The Agent shall provide, upon written request, an accounting to me (if capable), to any guardian or conservator, to my personal representative upon my death, or to any other person authorized by Court order.

### X. RELIANCE BY THIRD PARTIES
Third parties may rely upon the authority of the Agent. I agree to indemnify third parties that in good faith accept and rely upon this Power of Attorney, consistent with M.G.L. c. 190B, § 5-505. A copy of this instrument shall have the same force and effect as the original.

### XI. REVOCATION OF PRIOR INSTRUMENTS
I hereby revoke any and all prior Powers of Attorney executed by me that are inconsistent with this instrument.

### XII. GOVERNING LAW
This instrument shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, specifically M.G.L. c. 190B, Article V, Part 5.

### SIGNATURE AND NOTARIZATION

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have executed this Durable Power of Attorney on this _____ day of ________________, 20___.

_______________________________
[Principal full legal name], Principal

COMMONWEALTH OF MASSACHUSETTS
[COUNTY] COUNTY, SS.

On this _____ day of ________________, 20___, before me, the undersigned notary public, personally appeared [Principal], proved to me through satisfactory evidence of identification, which was [form of ID], to be the person whose name is signed on the preceding or attached document and acknowledged to me that he/she signed it voluntarily for its stated purpose.

_______________________________
Notary Public
My commission expires: ____________

### ACCEPTANCE BY AGENT (OPTIONAL BUT RECOMMENDED)

I, [Primary Agent name], hereby accept appointment as attorney-in-fact for [Principal name] under the above Durable Power of Attorney, and agree to act in accordance with the standards of conduct stated therein and with applicable law.

_______________________________
[Agent signature and date]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 190B, § 5-501` (definition + durability), `§ 5-505` (reliance by third parties). For the statutory short-form power of attorney, cite `M.G.L. c. 190B, Article V, Part 5`.
- Notarization IS REQUIRED for a Massachusetts Durable Power of Attorney, particularly to record real estate transactions. Witnesses are NOT required by statute but are good practice.
- 'Hot powers' (gifting, changing beneficiary designations, creating/amending trusts) MUST be expressly granted — the Agent does NOT have these powers by default.
- When `effectiveness` = `springing`, the incapacity-determination mechanism (two physicians) MUST be included; do not leave it to inference.
- MA has NOT adopted the 2006 Uniform Power of Attorney Act in its entirety — but most of its provisions are reflected in M.G.L. c. 190B, Article V, Part 5 (enacted 2009, effective 2012). Do NOT cite the 2006 UPOAA as Massachusetts law.
- The Agent should keep a contemporaneous record of actions (bank statements, receipts) — include the accounting obligation.
- Medicaid / MassHealth planning authority, when granted, should be specific enough to withstand institutional scrutiny (banks, nursing facilities often demand specific language).
- Target length: 4–8 pages.
hints: Massachusetts Durable Power of Attorney under M.G.L. c. 190B, Article V, Part 5. Durable = survives incapacity (§ 5-501). Two effectiveness modes: immediate OR springing (two-physician incapacity determination). Notarization REQUIRED. Hot powers (gifting per 26 USC § 2503(b), trust amendment, beneficiary designation changes, Medicaid planning per 42 USC § 1396p(c)) MUST be expressly granted. Third-party reliance and indemnity under § 5-505. MRUFADAA digital-asset authority (M.G.L. c. 190B, Art. VII). Agent accepts in writing. Primary + successor agents structure. NOT the 2006 UPOAA — MA has its own MUPC adoption.
